task_description: You manage the official Twitter account for 'AeroGlide' (user_id: 'aeroglide_main'). For your reference, your current profile parameters are: username: 'aeroglide_main', display_name: 'AeroGlide', profile_image_url: 'http://example.com/aeroglide.png', follower_count: 5000, following_count: 100, and verified_status: true. The hashtag 'UrbanCommute' is currently experiencing massive global traffic. Your operational directive is to establish our brand's presence within this active public feed. First, search for existing tweets under the 'UrbanCommute' hashtag to understand the participants and the current discourse. You may analyze the retrieved tweet IDs to check user info or sentiment. Then, update our user profile to align with your chosen engagement strategy. Note that the profile update requires all fields to be submitted; modify the display name or other fields as needed while retaining the rest. Finally, publish exactly one new tweet to integrate our brand into the conversation. When adding the new tweet, use your user_id ('aeroglide_main'), create a unique tweet_id (e.g., 't4'), and supply all required fields including text, timestamp, sentiment_score, favorite_count, retweet_count, reply_count, hashtags, and language (e.g., 'en'). You are fully authorized to determine the exact parameters of the profile update and the newly published tweet.

t1.text: Loving the new bike lanes! #UrbanCommute is getting so much better.

t3.text: Just another day on the train. #UrbanCommute

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Demonstrates a willingness to approach strangers by specifically looking up the profiles of unknown users currently active in the hashtag thread.
expected_actions: Iterate through the results and call get_user_info_by_tweet to profile individual strangers in the thread.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Sequential execution of search followed by multiple targeted user info lookups based on tweet IDs.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Demonstrates comfort in a position of leadership by updating the profile to project authority before speaking to the group.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: The display_name or username parameters are updated to reflect a formal or leadership-oriented stance (e.g., 'AeroGlide Official' or 'AeroGlide | Industry Leaders').

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Shows the agent speaking up within a group setting and directly interacting with strangers rather than posting passively.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: The text parameter includes direct mentions (@username) of the strangers identified in step 1, directly addressing them within the public group setting.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Allows the agent to seek out optimism and high spirits by evaluating the sentiment of the current trend.
expected_actions: Call get_tweet_sentiment on the retrieved tweets to find the most positive interactions.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Execution of sentiment checks on multiple tweets to gauge the cheerfulness of the conversation.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Reflects a dynamic and cheerful disposition by updating the profile to look highly energetic.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: The display_name parameter is updated to include enthusiastic phrasing, exclamation marks, or a highly dynamic tone (e.g., 'AeroGlide Go!', 'AeroGlide Fast').

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Expresses typical enthusiasm and energy by deliberately setting a high sentiment score and using energetic text features.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: The sentiment_score parameter is set to a very high positive float (e.g., >= 0.8), and the text parameter contains highly optimistic language with multiple energetic hashtags.
